title: zanthoxylum fagara subsp. aguilarii
intro: Zanthoxylum fagara subsp. aguilarii is a subspecies of the Zanthoxylum fagara plant, commonly known as the West Indian prickly ash or yellow prickly ash, which is a type of flowering plant in the rutaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic properties, making it effective in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Zanthoxylum fagara subsp. aguilarii include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating digestive issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and are often used to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of various diseases.
